name: Moby Dick
primary_sources: Moby-Dick; or, The Whale by Herman Melville

visual_attributes.morphology: massive, elongated body typical of a sperm whale
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: larger than typical sperm whales, approximately 85 feet in length
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: typical sperm whale anatomy with a large head and narrow lower jaw

domain_specific_visual.literary_symbolism: represents the unconquerable and mysterious forces of nature
domain_specific_visual.narrative_role: antagonist in Captain Ahab's quest for vengeance

functional_attributes.primary_function: symbol of nature's power and mystery

domain_specific_functional.literary_role: drives the plot and themes of obsession and revenge
domain_specific_functional.symbolic_function: represents the futility of human struggle against nature

relationships.description: Moby Dick is the object of Ahab's vengeful pursuit.

contextual_attributes.origin: fictional representation inspired by real-life albino whale Mocha Dick
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the ultimate challenge and mystery of the sea
primary_sources: Moby-Dick; or, The Whale by Herman Melville

contrastive_constraints: NOT a benign or friendly creature
